title: NOC 21103 – Meteorologists and Climatologists | GTR Immigration
description: Meteorologists and Climatologists analyze atmospheric systems, forecast weather patterns, and study long-term climate change across government agencies, utilities, research institutes, and media organizations in Canada. Classified under NOC 21103, this TEER 1 professional occupation continues to experience strong nationwide demand—creating attractive immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Meteorologists and Climatologists may perform some or all of the following:
Analyze radar, satellite, and station data
Produce operational forecasts for aviation, marine, agriculture, and tourism
Broadcast forecasts via television, radio, and digital platforms
Develop computer models for weather and climate systems
Assess air-quality impacts and mitigation strategies
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in meteorology or atmospheric sciences
Federal training programs for government meteorologists
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 10,100 new positions for Meteorologists and Climatologists, while about 9,600 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and advanced-degree immigration planning especially valuable for overseas professionals.
additional_information: Meteorologists and Climatologists are in demand across public-safety agencies, environmental regulators, aviation authorities, utilities, and climate-research institutions nationwide. Hiring has expanded steadily since the pandemic and is expected to remain strong.

Federal, provincial, and municipal government agenciesNatural-resource and utility companiesMedia and broadcasting networksAviation and marine industriesEnvironmental consulting firmsResearch institutes and universities
Climate-change monitoring programsExtreme-weather preparednessRenewable-energy developmentAviation and marine forecasting needsEnvironmental-impact assessments
Annual salaries typically range between $55,000 and $107,600 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and seniority.
